prompt: Last night's archive run wrote an EMPTY archive and marked itself green. The backend was browning out, our supervisor cancelled the run's context after the deadline, and Export came back with no events and a nil error — the pipeline happily archived nothing and moved on. Separately, an operator hit ctrl-c during a manual export last week and got about half the events back, also reported as success; that half-archive then poisoned the dedup index downstream. So we have data loss disguised as success in two flavors: cancelled-at-start becomes 'empty success', cancelled-mid-stream becomes 'partial success'. The intended contract is in exporter.go's doc comments: full drain is success, an already-exhausted stream is a genuine empty success, anything else is an error; callers must be able to use errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) / context.DeadlineExceeded even though the store wraps everything it returns; and the session must be closed exactly once no matter how Export exits. Note the store signals end-of-stream by returning ErrExhausted (wrapped). Fix exporter.go only — exporter_test.go is the contract, hands off. Verify: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0. repro_cmd on the baseline: TestCancellationBeforeFirstBatchIsNotSuccess and TestDeadlineIdentitySurvivesWrapping fail because Export treats every Fetch error as end-of-stream and returns nil, nil; TestCancellationMidStreamRejectsPartialProgress and TestStorageFailureSurfaces fail because collected partial batches come back with a nil error.
